lessonTitle: The Fair and the Coin: The Birth of Money

questions.question: In closed patriarchal-communal societies before trade existed, who controlled the main portion of resources?
questions.correct_answer: Heads of families, clans, or tribal chiefs controlled the main portion of resources.

questions.question: What were the first coins created from and why were more durable materials chosen?
questions.correct_answer: First coins were created from copper, bronze, silver, or gold because tags made from fragile materials were vulnerable to counterfeiting.

questions.question: How did seasonal fairs emerge according to the lesson?
questions.correct_answer: Seasonal fairs emerged when merchants began meeting at intersections of caravan routes for joint rest stops, where they exchanged goods and information and arranged to meet in the next season.

questions.question: What was the special entrance for buyers called and why did it have this name?
questions.correct_answer: The special entrance for buyers was called the 'needle's eye' because of its similarity to the shape of the thread holder on old needles.

questions.question: How did the network banking system develop from individual coin owners?
questions.correct_answer: Coin owners expanded by renting space in other markets to open branches of their cashier's office (exchange points), which created the network banking system.
